message:
perf(daemon): optimize connection clone in UDS client

The UdsDaemonClient was wastefully cloning the Unix socket stream twice
when reusing a cached connection: once to put back in the cache and once
to return. This created unnecessary syscalls and potential race conditions.

The fix reorders operations to clone only once:
1. Clone the stream to put back in the cache
2. Return the original stream directly (no second clone)

This is safe because after we take() the stream from the Option, we own
it exclusively. By returning the original and caching the clone, we:
- Reduce syscalls by 50% for cached connection reuse
- Simplify the error handling (single clone error path)
- Make the ownership semantics clearer

The behavioral change is minimal: the caller gets the original stream
while the cache holds a clone, versus the previous behavior where both
were clones. Both approaches maintain valid file descriptors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
